methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Electra, a Series B climate and energy company, has raised $299M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA), all operating in the Climate & Energy sector at equivalent or adjacent stages. The private peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring to $299M in total raised and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $568M. The peer-median method across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range of $120M to $5.0B. The wide peer range reflects material heterogeneity in capital intensity and commercialization progress within frontier climate tech.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $302M (low), $462M (median), to $653M (high). The median estimate of $462M sits meaningfully above the peer median of $355M, reflecting the company's above-median capital raise. Key uncertainties include technology maturity and path-to-profitability relative to the peer cohort, particularly given the outlier valuation of Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B. The wide range indicates meaningful dispersion in how frontier-stage climate companies are valued in current market conditions.
